message:
feat(fft): add rfft with norm variants parity (franken_numpy-1nir)

Wire numpy.fft.rfft through the fnp-python PyO3 surface, covering the
four norm contracts NumPy supports:

- None / "backward" → no scaling (default)
- "ortho"           → 1/sqrt(n) scaling
- "forward"         → 1/n scaling
- anything else     → ValueError matching NumPy's exact message text

Scaling is applied post-transform on the interleaved complex output so
the inner UFuncArray::rfft implementation stays scale-agnostic. A new
build_numpy_complex_array_from_interleaved helper reshapes the trailing
(..., 2) interleaved layout into a numpy.complex128 array and preserves
leading dimensions via reshape when the logical shape has rank > 1.

Tests exercise all four norm variants against numpy.fft.rfft with the
existing assert_array_matches_numpy oracle, plus the "bad norm"
rejection path. Also reflows the save/load NaN/Inf/-0.0 fixture array
onto multiple lines so rustfmt stops churning it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
